observed: The committed discovery module scans operations/queue-updates for regular files matching M-<digits>*.json.
Discovered paths are sorted deterministically before their UTF-8 contents are supplied to the existing report composer.
The returned discovery metadata explicitly records that history was not modified.
inferred: Separating repository discovery from report composition keeps filesystem scope testable and prevents the discovery layer from silently redefining continuity validity.
proposed: Add a CLI wrapper that invokes buildRepositoryContinuityInventory with buildContinuityInventoryReport and writes a generated report artifact in CI.
superseded: Requiring callers to manually enumerate continuity queue files before an inventory can be composed.
unresolved: full repository inventory artifact generated from a checked-out default branch
classification of intentional versus accidental forks
semantic matching for differently worded but equivalent unresolved claims

verification.route: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical source and test content before commit

privacy_boundary: Public repository paths, abstract queue metadata, and synthetic test records only. Raw private chat, health and pet-health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
